mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS trends down, the discount rate on future earnings falls and equity valuations re-rate upward, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month. The effect is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because disinflation keeps real yields from rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during 3-month FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL inflation is flat to falling.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent local range signals deteriorating labor demand and impending earnings downgrades, so S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next month. The signal should activate only when payroll growth also weakens, confirming broad labor softness rather than a data glitch.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal remains null when PAYEMS growth is simultaneously weakening.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial activity and better cyclical earnings momentum, so equities should rise over the next quarter. The effect fails when production improves only after a prior recessionary collapse, because rebound noise dominates forward returns.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60-day SPX returns do not occur more often after rising INDPRO than after flat or falling INDPRO, or if the relationship disappears in non-recovery expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both improve, household and retail demand are strengthening together, which supports revenue growth expectations and pushes S&P 500 higher over the next month. The joint condition matters because either series alone can reflect sector-specific noise without broad demand confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-HOUST improvement does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal is not followed by above-baseline equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening surprise occurs when FEDFUNDS is elevated while CPIAUCSL is still firm, because real rates rise and financial conditions compress valuations. In that regime, S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate, still-inflationary periods do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction adds no predictive value over either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST signals weaker housing-led demand and tighter consumer activity ahead, so equity returns should be negative over the next month. The effect is expected to be clearest when RSAFS is also slowing, indicating broad demand deceleration rather than a housing-only dip.
failure_condition: Reject if falling HOUST is not associated with negative 20-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal disappears when RSAFS confirms a broader slowdown.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is accelerating while UNRATE is stable or falling, labor income growth is supporting consumption and profit expectations, which should lift the S&P 500 over the next quarter. This mechanism fails if payroll gains are concentrated in a period of rising unemployment, because then the employment signal is not translating into demand.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll acceleration with flat or falling unemployment does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if rising UNRATE neutralizes the effect entirely.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in INDPRO after a prior growth phase signals an industrial earnings reset and weaker cyclical margins, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next month. The signal is regime-specific and is most informative when GDP growth has already turned sluggish, because then the production downturn reflects true cyclical weakening rather than temporary volatility.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive INDPRO declines do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the pattern fails specifically when GDPC1 growth is already weak.
